paper_title: A Measure of Countries' Distance to Frontier Based on Comparative Advantage
research_answer: The distance-to-frontier ranking, which is based purely on comparative advantage in trade data, correlates remarkably well with a range of standard economic development measures. Its correlation with GDP per capita is 0.83 — matching exactly the correlation with capital per worker (0.83) and close to government effectiveness (0.86), R&D intensity (0.80), and human capital (0.77). What's striking is that a measure derived entirely from a country's export structure — with no direct input of income, investment, or policy data — achieves predictive accuracy on par with fundamental drivers of growth like physical and human capital. This suggests export complexity serves as a powerful summary statistic of a country's productive capabilities.
supporting_quotes: The ranking is very robust and highly correlated with GDP per capita (~.83).
log(RD) is the 19-year centered moving average of a country's log share of GDP invested in R&D
GE is 'government effectiveness' from the Worldwide Governance Indicators
log(HC) is the log Human Capital Index
log(K/L) is the log capital stock per capita

data_points.metric: rank correlation with GDP per capita
rank correlation with capital per worker
